summary: Verify canonical repos, sites, docs, and status for Kjolstad lab software projects

result.name: TACO — The Tensor Algebra Compiler
result.summary: TACO (the Tensor Algebra Compiler) is an MIT-licensed C++ library and code generator that compiles tensor algebra expressions over dense and sparse tensors into optimized C and CUDA kernels for CPUs and GPUs.
result.statusNote: GitHub repo (tensor-compiler/taco) is public and not archived, MIT license, ~1.4k stars, ~2,800 commits on master; latest tagged GitHub release is "First taco" dated 2019-05-30. The official site tensor-compiler.org resolves to GitHub Pages and serves the correct TACO content, including the online web tool at /codegen (title "Web Tool | TACO: The Tensor Algebra Compiler"). Caveat for linking: as of this check (2026-07-16), https://tensor-compiler.org presents a TLS certificate that does not match the domain (a github.com cert via GitHub Pages), so HTTPS currently triggers a browser certificate warning.

result.name: Simit
result.summary: Simit is a programming language for physical simulation and computing on sparse systems that lets a program be viewed simultaneously as a hypergraph with local update functions and as global vectors, matrices, and tensors, compiling via LLVM to CPU and GPU code.
result.statusNote: Last code push 2019-05-17 per GitHub API; repository is public and not archived (460 stars).

result.name: Copy-and-Patch Compilation (PochiVM)
result.summary: A fast baseline compilation technique that generates machine code by copying pre-built binary code stencils and patching in constants and addresses instead of running an optimizing compiler backend, implemented in the PochiVM C++ code-generation framework.
result.statusNote: Not archived; last commit 2024-08-19 per GitHub API (107 stars). Owned by co-author Haoran Xu (GitHub: sillycross).

result.name: Deegen
result.summary: Deegen is a meta-compiler (VM generator) that takes a dynamic language's bytecode semantics written as C++ functions and automatically generates a two-tier execution engine consisting of a high-performance interpreter and a Copy-and-Patch baseline JIT, and is used to build the LuaJIT Remake Lua VM.
result.statusNote: Repo last updated February 2025 per GitHub; no dedicated "deegen" repo exists — Deegen lives in the luajit-remake repository.

result.name: Etch (Indexed Streams)
result.summary: Etch is a Lean 4 compiler and formalization implementing indexed streams, an operational intermediate representation for the fused execution of contraction programs spanning sparse tensor algebra and relational algebra.
result.statusNote: Not archived; last commit June 2024 (pushed_at 2024-06-13) per GitHub API.

result.name: Legate Sparse
result.summary: A Legate library providing a distributed and accelerated drop-in replacement for the scipy.sparse library, running unmodified sparse SciPy programs across clusters of CPUs and GPUs on top of the Legate runtime.
result.statusNote: Active; latest release v26.02.00 on 2026-02-14 with active branch-26.02 per GitHub. Repo describes project as alpha, supporting a subset of scipy.sparse (CSR and DIA formats).

result.name: SAM (Sparse Abstract Machine)
result.summary: The Sparse Abstract Machine (SAM) is a streaming-dataflow abstract machine model and intermediate representation for compiling sparse tensor algebra to reconfigurable and fixed-function spatial dataflow accelerators, and the repository provides the SAM IR, its compiler front-end, and a Python cycle-tracking simulator (with the Custard compiler shipped as a companion C++ fork of taco).
result.statusNote: Repository last updated Oct 2025 per GitHub; MIT-licensed, ~836 commits. Companion compiler at https://github.com/weiya711/custard (last updated Feb 2024). No separate "comal" repository was found owned by the authors; the SAM simulator lives inside weiya711/sam.

result.name: Scorch
result.summary: A Python library that provides sparse tensors and JIT-compiled kernels with a PyTorch-compatible API, using a sparse-tensor-algebra compiler stack that automates loop ordering, tiling, and format inference for sparse ML workloads.
result.statusNote: Public and not archived; most recent push 2026-07-17 and repo updated 2026-06-30 per GitHub API; Python (with C++), 8 stars, no license file present.

result.name: DISTAL (The Distributed Tensor Algebra Compiler)
result.summary: DISTAL is a compiler for dense distributed tensor algebra (extended to sparse tensors by SpDISTAL) that lets users independently specify data distribution and computation distribution via separate scheduling languages and compiles the result to the Legion distributed task-based runtime, implemented as a distributed extension of the TACO tensor algebra compiler.
result.statusNote: Code lives on the DISTAL branch of rohany/taco; most recent commit on that branch is November 2022 per GitHub.

result.name: Finch (built on Looplets)
result.summary: Finch is a Julia-based compiler for sparse and structured multidimensional array programs, built on Looplets, a language for structured coiteration introduced by Willow Ahrens et al. at CGO 2023.
result.statusNote: Active and not archived per GitHub; MIT license; latest release v1.4.0 dated 2026-07-15; ~4,680 commits on main.

result.name: Mosaic
result.summary: Mosaic is a tensor algebra compiler, built on top of TACO, that binds tensor (sub-)expressions to external functions from other tensor algebra libraries and compilers—substituting the bound expressions with those calls and generating the remaining code—with an automatic search to map expressions onto registered functions and verification of both explicit and searched bindings.

result.name: DAM: The Dataflow Abstract Machine Simulator Framework
result.summary: A Rust framework for building high-performance parallel simulators of dataflow-like systems, modeling computation as contexts (graph nodes) communicating over latency-bearing channels (edges).
result.statusNote: Dual-licensed Apache-2.0/MIT; latest release v0.1.0 September 2024 per GitHub, 266 commits

result.name: Burrito
result.summary: Burrito is a compiler for a sparse array language that supports shape operators such as reshaping and concatenating sparse arrays alongside compute operators, generating fused code that iterates over reshaped views of irregular sparse data structures without materializing intermediates.
result.statusNote: GitHub repo is the OOPSLA 2024 artifact; "OOPSLA 2024 Release" published August 27, 2024 per GitHub.

result.name: RECUMA (RECUrrence computation MAchine)
result.summary: RECUMA is a compiler that lowers user-specified systems of recurrence equations, together with data-structure and scheduling directives, into imperative C loop nests over dense and sparse arrays.
result.statusNote: Evaluation artifact archived on Zenodo as a Docker container (https://zenodo.org/records/10774458, 2024); no public source-code repository found on the authors' GitHub (github.com/shivsundram enumerated via API, checked 2026-07). Follow-on work REPTILE (OOPSLA 2025) is DOI 10.1145/3763074.
